name: Proximity POP
description: The Proximity POP connects points to nearby points in its first input within a specified near/far distance. If a second input is connected, it connects points of the second input with points of the first input that are in-range. Two parameters set the Maximum Distance and the Minimum Distance between points to connect. You can limit how many lines each point can connect from. The Uniform Distribution parameter gives a more random distribution of connecting points. You can ensure each pair of points have at most one line between them. When there are two inputs to Proximity, you can output (via the Output menu) the connecting lines, or simply point primitives of the first input that are within range of the second input. When outputting lines, you can include primitive attributes LineDir and LineLength. When outputting points you can also output all the attributes of the matching point as separate new attributes (EndP, EndN, EndTex etc) by select End Point Attributes. Each connecting primitive is a 2-point "line primitive", not a line strip. See also [[Neighbor POP]], [[Line Metrics POP]]

parameters.name: Maximum Distance
parameters.label: Maximum Distance
parameters.description: Maximum distance from current point for candidate points to create lines to.

parameters.name: Minimum Distance
parameters.label: Minimum Distance
parameters.description: Minimum distance from current point for candidate points to create lines to.

parameters.name: Max Lines per Point
parameters.label: Max Lines per Point
parameters.description: Specifies the max number of lines emanating from each point in the first input.

parameters.name: Uniform Distribution
parameters.label: Uniform Distribution
parameters.description: Allows evenly distributed selection among nearby points, rather than defaulting to the first ones found.

parameters.name: Max Temp Neighbors
parameters.label: Max Temp Neighbors
parameters.description: Used internally to store candidate points to create lines to before removing some to reach the max number of lines per point.

parameters.name: Duplicate Lines
parameters.label: Duplicate Lines
parameters.description: Determines what to do with duplicated proximity lines

parameters.name: Num Hash Buckets
parameters.label: Num Hash Buckets
parameters.description: The number of buckets the points will be sorted in based on their position. A good heuristic is to choose it to be close to the number of points.

parameters.name: Output
parameters.label: Output
parameters.description: Whether to output points with attributes or lines.

parameters.name: Line Direction
parameters.label: Line Direction
parameters.description: Enable addition of line direction attribute.

parameters.name: Line Length
parameters.label: Line Length
parameters.description: Enable addition of line length attribute.

parameters.name: End Point Attributes
parameters.label: End Point Attributes
parameters.description: Enable outputting end point attributes from second input

parameters.name: Origin
parameters.label: Origin
parameters.description: Sets whether the lines origin comes from the first input or the second input.

parameters.name: Remove Unused Points
parameters.label: Remove Unused Points
parameters.description: Removes unused points not referenced by primitives.

parameters.name: Copy Topology Info Back to CPU
parameters.label: Copy Topology Info Back to CPU
parameters.description: Enable copying the point count and topology information held on the GPU to the CPU.

_provenance.fact: parameters and description scraped from the live Proximity_POP wiki page ({{Parameter}}/{{ParameterItem}} templates)
